name: Algod Client Package Examples
description: Create runnable examples demonstrating the @algorandfoundation/algokit-algod-client package. IMPORTANT: After implementing each example, you MUST run it to verify it works: `npx tsx <example>/index.ts`. The algod-client package provides a typed, promise-based client for the Algorand algod REST API.

userStories.title: Infrastructure Setup - Shared Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared utilities and constants for all algod_client examples so that examples have consistent setup and helper functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/shared/constants.ts with ALGOD_CONFIG and KMD_CONFIG for LocalNet connection

Create examples/algod_client/shared/utils.ts with: printHeader(), printStep(), printInfo(), printSuccess(), printError(), formatAlgo(), shortenAddress(), createAlgodClient(), createKmdClient(), getFundedAccount(), createRandomAccount()
Create examples/algod_client/package.json referencing file:../../dist for @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils
Create examples/algod_client/tsconfig.json with ESM module settings
Create examples/algod_client/README.md documenting all algod_client examples
npm install runs without errors in examples/algod_client directory
TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit

userStories.title: Node Health and Status Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to check node health and status using healthCheck(), ready(), status(), and statusAfterBlock().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/01-node-health-status/index.ts
Demonstrate healthCheck() to check if the node is healthy (returns null if healthy)
Demonstrate ready() to check if the node is ready to accept transactions
Demonstrate status() to get current node status including lastRound, catchupTime, etc.
Demonstrate statusAfterBlock(round) to wait for a specific block
Display relevant status fields: lastRound, catchupTime, timeSinceLastRound, hasSyncedSinceStartup
Handle and display any error conditions gracefully
Run the example with `npx tsx 01-node-health-status/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Version and Genesis Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get node version information and genesis configuration using version() and genesis().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/02-version-genesis/index.ts
Demonstrate version() to get algod version information
Display version fields: build.major, build.minor, build.buildNumber, build.commitHash, genesisId, genesisHash
Demonstrate genesis() to get the full genesis configuration
Display genesis fields: network, proto, alloc (accounts), fees, rwd, timestamp
Show how to decode the base64 genesis hash
Run the example with `npx tsx 02-version-genesis/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Ledger Supply Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get ledger supply information using supply().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/03-ledger-supply/index.ts
Demonstrate supply() to get total money supply information
Display totalMoney (total Algos in the network)
Display onlineMoney (Algos in online accounts for consensus)
Calculate and display the percentage of Algos that are online
Format all amounts in both microAlgos and Algos for readability
Run the example with `npx tsx 03-ledger-supply/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Account Information Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get comprehensive account information using accountInformation(), accountApplicationInformation(), and accountAssetInformation().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/04-account-info/index.ts
Demonstrate accountInformation(address) to get full account details
Display account fields: address, balance, minBalance, status, round
Show assets held by the account (assetHolding) if any
Show applications created by the account (createdApps) if any
Show applications the account has opted into (appsLocalState) if any
Demonstrate accountApplicationInformation(address, appId) for specific app info
Demonstrate accountAssetInformation(address, assetId) for specific asset info
Use a funded account from LocalNet for testing
Run the example with `npx tsx 04-account-info/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Transaction Parameters Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get suggested transaction parameters using suggestedParams() and transactionParams().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/05-transaction-params/index.ts
Demonstrate suggestedParams() to get parameters for building transactions
Display suggested params: fee, flatFee, firstRound, lastRound, genesisID, genesisHash
Explain each parameter's purpose in transaction construction
Show how firstRound and lastRound define the transaction's validity window
Demonstrate how to customize parameters (e.g., setting a specific fee)
Run the example with `npx tsx 05-transaction-params/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Send and Confirm Transaction Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to send transactions and wait for confirmation using sendRawTransaction() and pendingTransactionInformation().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/06-send-transaction/index.ts
Create a simple payment transaction using AlgorandClient
Sign the transaction with a funded account
Demonstrate sendRawTransaction(signedTxn) to submit the transaction
Demonstrate pendingTransactionInformation(txId) to check transaction status
Implement a waitForConfirmation loop using pendingTransactionInformation
Display the confirmed transaction details: confirmedRound, txn, fee
Handle and display any transaction errors (poolError)
Run the example with `npx tsx 06-send-transaction/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Pending Transactions Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to query pending transactions using pendingTransactions() and pendingTransactionsByAddress().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/07-pending-transactions/index.ts
Demonstrate pendingTransactions() to get all pending transactions in the pool
Demonstrate pendingTransactionsByAddress(address) to get pending txns for a specific account
Show the structure of pending transaction data
Display totalTransactions count from the pool
Handle the case where there are no pending transactions
Optionally submit a transaction and immediately query for it in the pending pool
Run the example with `npx tsx 07-pending-transactions/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Block Data Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to retrieve block information using block(), blockHash(), and blockTxIds().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/08-block-data/index.ts
Demonstrate block(round) to get full block data for a specific round
Display block fields: round, timestamp, previousBlockHash, seed, txns
Demonstrate blockHash(round) to get just the block hash
Demonstrate blockTxIds(round) to get transaction IDs in a block
Show how to navigate block structure and access transaction data
Get the latest round from status() and fetch that block
Run the example with `npx tsx 08-block-data/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Asset Information Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get asset information using assetById().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/09-asset-info/index.ts
Create a test asset using AlgorandClient for setup
Demonstrate assetById(assetId) to get asset information
Display asset params: creator, total, decimals, unitName, assetName, url, metadataHash
Display asset addresses: manager, reserve, freeze, clawback
Show current round the data is valid for
Handle the case where asset is not found
Run the example with `npx tsx 09-asset-info/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Application Information Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get application information using applicationById().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/10-application-info/index.ts
Deploy a simple test application using AlgorandClient for setup
Demonstrate applicationById(appId) to get application information
Display application params: creator, approvalProgram, clearStateProgram
Display state schema: globalStateSchema, localStateSchema
Show globalState if the application has any global state
Handle the case where application is not found
Run the example with `npx tsx 10-application-info/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Application Boxes Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to query application boxes using applicationBoxes() and applicationBoxByName().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/11-application-boxes/index.ts
Deploy an application that uses box storage
Create several boxes with different names and values
Demonstrate applicationBoxes(appId) to list all boxes for an application
Display box names from the boxes array
Demonstrate applicationBoxByName(appId, boxName) to get a specific box value
Show how to decode box name from base64 and display the value
Handle the case where application has no boxes
Run the example with `npx tsx 11-application-boxes/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: TEAL Compile and Disassemble Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to compile and disassemble TEAL programs using tealCompile() and tealDisassemble().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/12-teal-compile/index.ts
Demonstrate tealCompile(source) to compile TEAL source code to bytecode
Compile a simple approval program (e.g., '#pragma version 10\nint 1')
Display the compiled result: hash, result (base64 bytecode)
Show with sourcemap option to get detailed compilation info
Demonstrate tealDisassemble(bytecode) to convert bytecode back to TEAL
Compare original source with disassembled output
Handle and display compilation errors for invalid TEAL
Run the example with `npx tsx 12-teal-compile/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Transaction Simulation Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to simulate transactions using simulateTransactions() and simulateRawTransactions().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/13-simulation/index.ts
Create a payment transaction for simulation
Demonstrate simulateRawTransactions(signedTxns) to simulate signed transactions
Display simulation results: wouldSucceed, fixedSigner results
Show transaction group details from the simulation
Demonstrate simulation with extra budget options
Show how simulation can be used to estimate fees and detect errors
Simulate a failing transaction and display the failure reason
Run the example with `npx tsx 13-simulation/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Ledger State Deltas Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get ledger state deltas using ledgerStateDelta(), ledgerStateDeltaForTransactionGroup(), and transactionGroupLedgerStateDeltasForRound().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/14-state-deltas/index.ts
Submit a transaction that modifies state (e.g., payment changes balances)
Demonstrate ledgerStateDelta(round) to get state changes for a round
Display account deltas showing balance changes
Demonstrate ledgerStateDeltaForTransactionGroup(txId) to get deltas for a specific transaction group
Demonstrate transactionGroupLedgerStateDeltasForRound(round) to get all transaction group deltas in a round
Show the structure of state delta data (accounts modified, new values)
Note: These endpoints may require node configuration to enable
Run the example with `npx tsx 14-state-deltas/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Transaction Proof Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get transaction proofs using transactionProof().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/15-transaction-proof/index.ts
Submit a transaction and wait for confirmation
Demonstrate transactionProof(round, txId) to get the Merkle proof for a transaction
Display proof fields: idx, proof, stibhash, treedepth, hashtype
Explain what transaction proofs are used for (light client verification)
Show the structure of the Merkle proof data
Handle errors gracefully if proof is not available
Run the example with `npx tsx 15-transaction-proof/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Light Block Header Proof Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get light block header proofs using lightBlockHeaderProof().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/16-lightblock-proof/index.ts
Demonstrate lightBlockHeaderProof(round) to get the proof for a block header
Display proof fields: index, proof, treedepth
Explain what light block header proofs are used for (state proof verification)
Show the relationship between block rounds and state proof intervals
Handle the case where proof is not available for the requested round
Note that light block header proofs are only available for specific rounds
Run the example with `npx tsx 16-lightblock-proof/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: State Proof Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to get state proofs using stateProof().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/17-state-proof/index.ts
Demonstrate stateProof(round) to get the state proof for a round
Explain what state proofs are (cryptographic proof of blockchain state)
Display state proof structure if available
Show the state proof interval and which rounds have proofs
Handle the case where state proof is not yet available
Note: State proofs are generated periodically, not for every round
Run the example with `npx tsx 17-state-proof/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: DevMode Timestamp Offset Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to manage block timestamp offset in DevMode using blockTimeStampOffset() and setBlockTimeStampOffset().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/18-devmode-timestamp/index.ts
Demonstrate blockTimeStampOffset() to get the current timestamp offset
Demonstrate setBlockTimeStampOffset(offset) to set a new timestamp offset
Show how timestamp offset affects block timestamps in DevMode
Get current block timestamp, set offset, trigger new block, compare timestamps
Reset the offset to 0 after the example
Note: These endpoints only work on DevMode LocalNet nodes
Handle errors gracefully if not running on DevMode
Run the example with `npx tsx 18-devmode-timestamp/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Sync Round Management Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to manage the sync round using syncRound(), setSyncRound(), and unsetSyncRound().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/19-sync-round/index.ts
Demonstrate syncRound() to get the minimum sync round the node will cache
Demonstrate setSyncRound(round) to set the minimum sync round
Demonstrate unsetSyncRound() to unset/reset the sync round
Explain what sync round means for node operation and data availability
Show how sync round affects which historical data is available
Handle errors gracefully if endpoints are not supported
Run the example with `npx tsx 19-sync-round/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Verification Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to run all algod_client examples and verify they work.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algod_client/verify-all.sh bash script
Script runs each example in order (01 through 19)
Script reports success/failure for each example
Script exits with non-zero code if any example fails
Add npm script 'verify-all' to package.json
All 19 examples pass when running ./verify-all.sh
